Aodhan Review - Empires & Puzzles Slayer Family Guide

Part wizard, part candle, all trouble. Aodhan brings the heat with self-healing stacks and those annoying Wisp Fiends that just won't go away. But in a world of super-creeps, is this slow-starting Slayer still hot stuff or just blowing smoke? We break down why he's the king of Bloody Battle.
Aodhan: The Burning Wick of the Slayer Family
Aodhan is a 4-star Epic Fire hero from the

family. Released back in 2021, he was one of the first accessible "Fiend Summoners" for many players, basically acting as a budget version of the legendary Elizabeth. He's a Wizard who loves to play the long game—starting slow like a damp matchstick but ramping up into a roaring bonfire if you let him live long enough. While his stats are showing their age, his kit offers a unique mix of sustain and anti-heal disruption that can still be a nightmare in specific formats.

Let’s be honest: Aodhan looks like he’s having a really intense bad hair day, or maybe he’s just the lead singer of a heavy metal band mid-solo. With his flaming vertical hair and glowing eyes, he gives off serious "human torch" energy. The floating fiery wisps in his hands are a nice touch, reminding us that he’s not here to punch you—he’s here to haunt you with little fire ghosts. He’s definitely one of the more "hot-headed" designs in the game (sorry, had to do it).

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Slayer family is all about momentum. They reward you for keeping them alive.

The family bonus gives a 50/75/100% chance (for 1/2/3 heroes) to generate a stack at the end of each turn. A stack grants health regeneration and mana generation. This means Aodhan naturally heals himself and gets faster every single turn, making him surprisingly tanky in prolonged fights.
This is huge. He takes 50% less damage from burns, poisons, and bleeds. This makes him a natural counter to DoT-heavy teams (looking at you, Barbarians and Poison dealers).

is... ironic? It prevents him from receiving fiends at the start of battle. It's situational. In a mirror match against another Aodhan or Elizabeth, it's great. Otherwise, it's a bit of a wasted slot compared to something like "Special Boost" or "Defense Up."

Upon release, Aodhan was celebrated as the "people's Elizabeth." He brought the powerful Fiend mechanic to the 4-star arena, dominating Bloody Battle tournaments where the fiends couldn't be healed off.

The game has gotten faster and hit harder. Aodhan's "ramp-up" style is risky in a meta where battles are often decided in the first 3-4 turns. His stats leave him vulnerable to being one-shot by modern snipers before he can get his stacks going.
He is no longer a general-purpose hero. However, he remains a top-tier pick for
(where his speed issue is solved) and
(where his fiends are permanent afflictions). He’s largely a benchwarmer for standard raids but a VIP for specific tournaments.
Aodhan needs help dealing damage, but he also needs to survive to build stacks.
Maximizes the initial hit damage and, crucially, increases the DoT from his Fiends (which scales off Attack).
Leaves him squishier, risking death before he reaches full Slayer speed.
Synergizes with his self-healing stacks to make him very hard to kill. Ensures he survives long enough to fire multiple times.
His special skill hits like a wet noodle, relying purely on the fiend's anti-heal utility.

is recommended. The primary value of the fiend is the damage and healing absorption. If he hits like a truck, the fiends become threats rather than annoyances. However, if you use him solely as a tank in Rush wars, Defense is viable.
Fiends absorb healing, effectively reducing the enemy's sustain.
In tournaments with no healing, fiends cannot be removed (unless by specific minion removal skills), dealing permanent damage every turn.
Regenerates HP every turn thanks to stacks, making him durable in attrition wars.
Becomes incredibly fast in long fights (Tower events, boss waves).
Starts at effectively Slow speed. If the board is bad, he dies before doing anything.
Low base stats make him vulnerable to modern nukers.
Heroes who destroy minions (like Grimble, Gobbler, or modern equivalents) can often clear fiends or exploit the minion slots.
While fiends aren't "cleansed," the healing absorption can be overcome by burst healers who overheal the cap.
Being Slow doesn't matter when everyone is Very Fast. He fires immediately, dumping fiends on everyone.
His best mode. The fiends stick forever and whittle down the enemy.
The stacks persist between waves (in some modes) or he has time to build them up, allowing him to be a machine gun boss killer.
Aodhan works best when he has time to breathe or when his speed is mitigated.
. Using 2 or 3 Slayers guarantees the family bonus stacks every turn, ramping everyone up significantly faster.

. While not a direct conflict, if your team fills the enemy slots with your own minions (not possible, but hypothetically), you can't cast fiends. More realistically, don't pair him with heroes who want the enemy to heal (is that a thing? No, not really).

If you pulled Aodhan early, he is a fantastic carry for map stages. His self-healing saves you potions, and his fiends will eventually kill tough bosses. Level him up, but prioritize his emblems for Attack to make map farming faster.

You likely have him sitting at 4/70. Is he worth the Limit Break? Only if you take 4-star tournaments seriously. A Double Limit Broken (LB2) Aodhan with 20 emblems is a monster in 4-star Rush and Bloody Battle, often being the deciding factor. If you ignore tournaments, he's safe to leave on the bench.
The community has always had a love-hate relationship with Slayer speed.

to making the speed mechanic reliable.

Aodhan is like that spicy salsa in the back of your fridge—you don't use it for every meal, but when it's Taco Tuesday (or Bloody Battle week), absolutely nothing else will do. He’s a specialized tool for punishing healers and surviving the apocalypse. While he’s no longer the shiny new toy, he burns bright where it counts.
Keep him. Max him for tournaments. Just don't expect him to win a sprint race against a Toon.
